scenario: A01_REJECT_REVIEW — Reject review or approval package before record reaches released/effective state — Customer/Supplier master and hold authority
required_actor: assigned reviewer or QA/domain approver independent from creator; policy owner: Commercial Lead + Supplier Quality Owner
precondition: record is not released/effective/posted; required review evidence is available; expected version token matches; affected group=G01_CUSTOMER_SUPPLIER_MASTER
required_reason: mandatory reject reason category + free-text rationale + affected section/requirement reference
required_evidence: review checklist, failed guard evidence, reviewer comment, linked record version, expected-version token
e_signature_meaning: review rejection / responsibility for review outcome when regulated or GxP-impacting
downstream_effect: prevent downstream release/dispatch/effectivity for QUO,CPO,SO,PO,RECEIPT,SHIPMENT,INVOICE,SCAR; notify originator and owner
compensation_or_reversal: return to prior editable state; do not delete review trail; next submission creates new review attempt
notification: originator, owner, next reviewer, impacted downstream planner/QA when applicable
frontend_state: reject action visible only in review state; required reason/evidence modal; released records show disabled action with correction/supersede alternatives; surface=authoritative record shell with hold banner, correction drawer, linked orders/lots impact tab
stop_rule: block if record is released/effective/posted, actor is creator-only, reason absent, audit unavailable, or expected version stale; group stop gate: master data SOD + duplicate/identity/terms/supplier-quality impact gate
notes: Reject is not a delete operation; it preserves the failed review decision as evidence. B09 source: B02-B08 planning outputs. Repo MOM GitHub not checked by user instruction; current repo state is treated as unverified.

scenario: A02_RETURN_FOR_CORRECTION — Return record/package for correction while keeping review trace intact — Customer/Supplier master and hold authority
required_actor: reviewer, QA reviewer, MRB chair, release reviewer or designated domain approver; policy owner: Commercial Lead + Supplier Quality Owner
precondition: record can still be corrected without invalidating released/posted downstream reliance; correction owner assigned; affected group=G01_CUSTOMER_SUPPLIER_MASTER
required_reason: mandatory correction reason and requested correction scope
required_evidence: return note, marked-up section/list, evidence deficiency, checklist finding, linked draft/version
e_signature_meaning: review return / correction requested; e-sign required if the returned package is GxP-impacting
downstream_effect: pause pending downstream QUO,CPO,SO,PO,RECEIPT,SHIPMENT,INVOICE,SCAR; no mutation to released/posted records
compensation_or_reversal: return to correction state; resubmission must preserve prior review cycle and version lineage
notification: originator, assigned owner, current reviewer group
frontend_state: return button in review queue with required deficiency list and correction owner assignment; surface=authoritative record shell with hold banner, correction drawer, linked orders/lots impact tab
stop_rule: block if return would silently alter signed/released evidence; use addendum or supersede instead; group stop gate: master data SOD + duplicate/identity/terms/supplier-quality impact gate
notes: Returned records carry full review history and do not erase failed/deficient state. B09 source: B02-B08 planning outputs. Repo MOM GitHub not checked by user instruction; current repo state is treated as unverified.

scenario: A03_PLACE_HOLD_OR_BLOCK — Place operational, quality, safety, supplier, customer, equipment, or release hold — Customer/Supplier master and hold authority
required_actor: authorized quality, production, inventory, supplier quality, EHS, maintenance or commercial hold owner; policy owner: Commercial Lead + Supplier Quality Owner
precondition: hold reason is within configured reason taxonomy; affected root has current state; initial impact scope selected; affected group=G01_CUSTOMER_SUPPLIER_MASTER
required_reason: mandatory hold reason with severity, suspected issue, scope and stop condition
required_evidence: hold request, defect/safety/supplier/customer/equipment evidence, affected lots/orders/equipment list, photos/attachments if applicable
e_signature_meaning: hold responsibility / quality or safety containment decision when regulated
downstream_effect: block or warning-gate affected QUO,CPO,SO,PO,RECEIPT,SHIPMENT,INVOICE,SCAR; freeze release/ship/consume/dispatch/use according to hold type
compensation_or_reversal: release hold only through explicit release action with resolution evidence; do not auto-expire critical holds
notification: owner, downstream planners/operators, QA, warehouse, EHS/maintenance when relevant
frontend_state: persistent hold banner, blocked action state, impact tab, linked open work/orders/lots/equipment; surface=authoritative record shell with hold banner, correction drawer, linked orders/lots impact tab
stop_rule: block command if hold reason, scope, owner, audit, or release criteria are missing; group stop gate: master data SOD + duplicate/identity/terms/supplier-quality impact gate
notes: Hold is a controlled authority state; it is not a comment or tag. B09 source: B02-B08 planning outputs. Repo MOM GitHub not checked by user instruction; current repo state is treated as unverified.

scenario: A04_RELEASE_HOLD — Release a previously placed controlled hold or block — Customer/Supplier master and hold authority
required_actor: authorized release owner independent from hold requester when required by risk class; policy owner: Commercial Lead + Supplier Quality Owner
precondition: resolution evidence, impact assessment and affected downstream re-check are complete; affected group=G01_CUSTOMER_SUPPLIER_MASTER
required_reason: mandatory release rationale and link to disposition/resolution
required_evidence: release checklist, investigation/disposition, CAPA/SCAR/NQ links, retest/reinspection, equipment RTS or training evidence

downstream_effect: recalculate authorization for QUO,CPO,SO,PO,RECEIPT,SHIPMENT,INVOICE,SCAR; notify previously blocked queues
compensation_or_reversal: if release is wrong, place new hold with release-reversal reason; never delete original hold/release history
notification: hold owner, affected downstream owners, planner/operator/warehouse/QA
frontend_state: release action shows impact-assessment checklist and prevents release until open blockers are cleared; surface=authoritative record shell with hold banner, correction drawer, linked orders/lots impact tab
stop_rule: block if open NQ/deviation/MRB/CAPA/OOT/safety/training/equipment blockers remain unresolved; group stop gate: master data SOD + duplicate/identity/terms/supplier-quality impact gate
notes: Hold release is high-risk and must be separately auditable from hold placement. B09 source: B02-B08 planning outputs. Repo MOM GitHub not checked by user instruction; current repo state is treated as unverified.

scenario: A05_CANCEL_UNSTARTED — Cancel draft, planned, or not-started record before execution/posting/release — Customer/Supplier master and hold authority
required_actor: record owner or authorized planner/buyer/commercial/maintenance owner; policy owner: Commercial Lead + Supplier Quality Owner
precondition: no posted transaction, consumed material, signed release evidence or active downstream execution relies on the record; affected group=G01_CUSTOMER_SUPPLIER_MASTER

required_evidence: cancel request, open downstream dependency check, remaining quantity/scope, stakeholder approval if externally committed
e_signature_meaning: cancellation approval when external/customer/supplier/GxP impact exists

compensation_or_reversal: posted/executed portion requires reversal/compensating transaction, not cancellation
notification: owner, planner, buyer, commercial contact, impacted operators/warehouse/QA as relevant
frontend_state: cancel disabled when started/posted/released; show 'reverse/correct/supersede' alternatives; surface=authoritative record shell with hold banner, correction drawer, linked orders/lots impact tab
stop_rule: block if any executed, posted, released or signed downstream reliance exists without explicit compensation model; group stop gate: master data SOD + duplicate/identity/terms/supplier-quality impact gate
notes: Cancel is not a correction tool for historical execution; it is only for future/non-started scope. B09 source: B02-B08 planning outputs. Repo MOM GitHub not checked by user instruction; current repo state is treated as unverified.

scenario: A06_VOID_DUPLICATE_OR_ERRONEOUS_UNRELIED — Void duplicate/erroneous record that has not been externally or regulatedly relied upon — Customer/Supplier master and hold authority
required_actor: record owner plus QA/system owner for regulated or audit-sensitive records; policy owner: Commercial Lead + Supplier Quality Owner

compensation_or_reversal: record remains visible as voided; never hard-delete; references must show void lineage

required_evidence: change request, redline/diff, impact assessment, training impact, validation impact, approval record, effectivity decision

compensation_or_reversal: create new revision/effective record; prior released version remains retained and viewable

stop_rule: block direct field edit of released/effective/signed record; require revision/supersede/addendum path; group stop gate: master data SOD + duplicate/identity/terms/supplier-quality impact gate
notes: This is the default path for released/signed controlled content. B09 source: B02-B08 planning outputs. Repo MOM GitHub not checked by user instruction; current repo state is treated as unverified.

scenario: A08_REOPEN_CLOSED — Reopen closed quality, release, audit, complaint, CAPA, maintenance, validation or EHS case — Customer/Supplier master and hold authority

required_evidence: new evidence, closure defect assessment, prior decision link, proposed review plan
e_signature_meaning: reopen approval / responsibility for reopening closed decision when regulated

compensation_or_reversal: closed status remains in history; reopened state creates new review cycle and cannot erase closure signature

frontend_state: reopen hidden/disabled until new-evidence reason and impact assessment are entered; surface=authoritative record shell with hold banner, correction drawer, linked orders/lots impact tab
stop_rule: block administrative reopen with no material evidence or when used to bypass correction/supersede controls; group stop gate: master data SOD + duplicate/identity/terms/supplier-quality impact gate

scenario: A09_REVERSE_POSTED_TRANSACTION — Reverse or compensate a posted transaction without editing original posting — Customer/Supplier master and hold authority

required_evidence: original transaction, corrected quantity/status, evidence of mistake, impact analysis, approval, reconciliation result
e_signature_meaning: transaction reversal approval / responsibility for compensation when regulated or release-impacting
downstream_effect: create compensation in all API/event/frontend/workflow/evidence roots that depend on platform control; preserve original transaction and display reversal chain
compensation_or_reversal: append compensating transaction; do not mutate/delete original
notification: transaction owner, inventory/warehouse, planner, QA, finance handoff owner if affected
frontend_state: original transaction locked; reverse/correct action creates linked compensation preview and impact warnings; surface=platform control shell with configuration history, incident addendum, rollback and affected service map
stop_rule: block if command attempts to overwrite posted transaction or cannot show compensation effect; group stop gate: platform correction/change must have owner, rollback, audit, affected consumer/service impact and security review where applicable
notes: Posted transaction corrections are ledger corrections, not edits. Use addendum/reopen/supersede rather than transaction reversal unless a ledger-like posted transaction exists. B09 source: B02-B08 planning outputs. Repo MOM GitHub not checked by user instruction; current repo state is treated as unverified.

scenario: A10_APPEND_ONLY_CORRECTION — Correct historical, executed, accepted or signed evidence through append-only amendment — Platform integration, IAM, API, event, tenant, notification and observability controls
required_actor: original actor if available plus reviewer/QA; independent approver for high-risk records; policy owner: Platform Lead + Security/Identity/SRE Owner
precondition: original record remains retained; correction reason, corrected value/text and affected evidence links are provided; affected group=G21_PLATFORM_INTEGRATION_CONTROL
required_reason: mandatory reason-for-change, original-vs-corrected value, discovery date, actual event date if late entry
required_evidence: correction addendum, source evidence, review approval, affected record list, audit trail reconstruction
e_signature_meaning: correction approval / responsibility for amended evidence when regulated
downstream_effect: mark linked all API/event/frontend/workflow/evidence roots that depend on platform control as impacted/review-required until assessment completes
compensation_or_reversal: add correction entry/addendum; preserve original value and show correction lineage
notification: record owner, QA/reviewer, affected downstream owners
frontend_state: edit fields locked; correction drawer captures original value, corrected value, reason, evidence and approval route; surface=platform control shell with configuration history, incident addendum, rollback and affected service map
stop_rule: block any correction that overwrites original record, removes signature, loses timestamp or lacks impact assessment; group stop gate: platform correction/change must have owner, rollback, audit, affected consumer/service impact and security review where applicable
notes: Append-only correction is the default for executed/accepted/signed evidence. B09 source: B02-B08 planning outputs. Repo MOM GitHub not checked by user instruction; current repo state is treated as unverified.

scenario: A11_LATE_ENTRY — Enter a late observation, missing entry or delayed evidence without falsifying contemporaneous timing — Platform integration, IAM, API, event, tenant, notification and observability controls
required_actor: person with direct knowledge plus supervisor/QA review according to risk class; policy owner: Platform Lead + Security/Identity/SRE Owner
precondition: late entry is clearly labeled; actual event time and entry time are both captured; rationale for delay exists; affected group=G21_PLATFORM_INTEGRATION_CONTROL
required_reason: mandatory late-entry reason and discovery/context statement
required_evidence: late-entry statement, source note/evidence, actual event time, entry time, reviewer approval
e_signature_meaning: late-entry attestation / review acceptance when regulated
downstream_effect: evaluate if late evidence affects all API/event/frontend/workflow/evidence roots that depend on platform control review, release, trend or investigation
compensation_or_reversal: add late entry as labeled evidence; do not backdate; do not alter original audit timestamp
notification: supervisor, QA/reviewer, affected record owner
frontend_state: late-entry banner with dual timestamp fields and blocking validation until reason/evidence is complete; surface=platform control shell with configuration history, incident addendum, rollback and affected service map
stop_rule: block if the UI/API can make late entry appear contemporaneous or omit actual entry time; group stop gate: platform correction/change must have owner, rollback, audit, affected consumer/service impact and security review where applicable
notes: Late entry may be acceptable only when transparent and reviewable. B09 source: B02-B08 planning outputs. Repo MOM GitHub not checked by user instruction; current repo state is treated as unverified.

scenario: A12_EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE — Emergency override of normal workflow due to urgent safety, containment, continuity or regulatory risk — Platform integration, IAM, API, event, tenant, notification and observability controls
required_actor: pre-authorized emergency role plus second approver/post-action QA review where feasible; policy owner: Platform Lead + Security/Identity/SRE Owner
precondition: defined emergency class, risk rationale, time-bound scope, no safer normal path, automatic post-action review task; affected group=G21_PLATFORM_INTEGRATION_CONTROL
required_reason: mandatory emergency reason, risk-of-delay statement and time limit
required_evidence: emergency justification, affected scope, actor authorization, post-action review task, remediation plan, evidence snapshot
e_signature_meaning: emergency authorization / accountability for override; post-action review approval
downstream_effect: force post-action impact assessment across all API/event/frontend/workflow/evidence roots that depend on platform control; create containment/review tasks
compensation_or_reversal: post-action review must either ratify, correct, hold, reverse, rework or open deviation/CAPA
notification: domain owner, QA, EHS if safety, validation/system owner, management escalation
frontend_state: high-friction override surface, two-step challenge, visible emergency banner, countdown/post-review queue; surface=platform control shell with configuration history, incident addendum, rollback and affected service map
stop_rule: block if override is used for convenience, missing time-bound scope, missing audit, or actor lacks emergency authority; group stop gate: platform correction/change must have owner, rollback, audit, affected consumer/service impact and security review where applicable
notes: Emergency override is allowed only to reduce greater immediate risk and must never become routine workflow. B09 source: B02-B08 planning outputs. Repo MOM GitHub not checked by user instruction; current repo state is treated as unverified.
